items.title: Baldur’s Gate 3 And Clair Obscur Actor Jennifer English Pulls Out Of New Fantasy Game Over Health Concerns
items.summary: She was set to star in the upcoming Tides of Annihilation
items.content:
Prolific video game actor Jennifer English will no longer play the main hero in Eclipse Glow Games’ upcoming hack-and-slash action-adventure Tides of Annihilation . The development team informed fans on July 17 that Baldur’s Gate 3 and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 actor would have to pull out of voicing protagonist Gwendolyn due to “personal health reasons.”

“She will continue to support the project in an advisory role. Earlier in June, Jennifer informed us that she would no longer be able to continue in the role,” Eclipse Glow Games wrote on X . “While we were, of course, saddened by her decision, we completely understood and respected Jennifer in prioritizing her well-being. We are also deeply grateful for the care and responsibility she showed throughout this process.”

The studio said it has already found a replacement for English with her help, but didn’t reveal who it was. The new actor will appear in the latest version of the game that players go hands-on with at Gamescom 2026 in August. “We have truly enjoyed this journey together, and wish Jennifer all the very best, and most importantly, good health and happiness in whatever comes next,” the development team wrote. “ To our players, thank you for your continued support and understanding. We’re truly grateful to have you on this journey with us.”

English is best known for her role as Shadowheart in Baldur’s Gate 3 and Maelle in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 . She’s also appeared in Elden Ring , Wuthering Waves , and most recently Marathon . Tides of Annihilation was first revealed during a 2025 PlayStation showcase and revolves around elements of Arthurian legend invading modern-day London for an urban fantasy action game that focuses on flashy arcade combat. It’s the latest single-player AAA blockbuster to come out of China, following in the footsteps of games like Black Myth Wukong .

English’s involvement had helped raise the profile of the game. Earlier this year s he won a BAFTA for her role in Clair Obscur .

items.title: Baldur's Gate 3 and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33's Jennifer English pulls out of fantasy RPG Tides of Annihilation: "Recently, I've found myself needing to slow down"
items.summary: The actor is staying on as an advisor moving forward
items.content:
The actor is staying on as an advisor moving forward

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Eclipse Glow Games) Copy link Facebook X Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Baldur's Gate 3 and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 star Jennifer English is pulling out of upcoming fantasy RPG Tides of Annihilation due to personal health reasons, and is handing her role over to another yet-unnamed actor while working as an advisor on the project.

Jennifer English has been involved in some of the best RPGs of recent years, lending her vocal chords to Baldur's Gate 3's Shadowheart, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 's Maelle, Elden Ring's Latenna, and much more. While Tides of Annihilation was hoping to cash in on the genre's lucky charm, things haven't turned out as planned.

"We have some sad news to share, that Jennifer English will be stepping away from her role as the English voice of Gwendolyn, the main protagonist in Tides of Annihilation due to personal health reasons," developer Eclipse Glow Games writes in a social media post. "She will continue to support the project in an advisory role."

We have some sad news to share, that Jennifer English will be stepping away from her role as the English voice of Gwendolyn, the main protagonist in Tides of Annihilation due to personal health reasons. She will continue to support the project in an advisory role. Earlier in… July 18, 2026

"During this transition, Jennifer remained incredibly supportive and, together with other members of our voice cast, helped us identify and connect with talented performers who could carry Gwendolyn forward while staying true to the character," the studio adds. "With Jennifer's support and recommendation, together with recommendations from other members of our voice cast, we've been fortunate to find an incredibly talented performer to carry Gwendolyn forward."

The Tencent -backed Eclipse Glow Games didn't name who will be voicing the game's protagonist moving forward, but we'll apparently find out by the time Tides of Annihilation's Gamescom 2026 demo is in the public's hands: "Players will hear her performance for the first time in the demo this August, as well as in select official updates shared across our channels during the same period."

As Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 further proves the Jen English effect, one co-star wants to join anything she does Clair Obscur Lune actor would be "delighted" to reprise her role, but she doesn't know of any plans Baldur's Gate 3 Karlach actor teases more about whether the "key cast" will return for the HBO sequel series Over on Instagram , English said she's "really grateful to have been part of Tides of Annihilation since the game's inception and to have worked with such lovely team." The actor also shared a bit more about what motivated her to take a step back from the project: "Recently, I've found myself needing to slow down and reassess the commitments I've taken on. After careful thought, I've realised I need to take time to rest and properly look after my mental and physical health."

Tides of Annihilation doesn't have a release window, but it'll be coming to PC, Xbox Series X|S and PS5 when it's out the oven. The game was originally set to have English star opposite her real-life partner Aliona Baranova in a shattered, twisted fantasy version of London.

Weekly digests, tales from the communities you love, and more

While you're here, why not check out the most exciting new games of 2026 and beyond.

Kaan freelances for various websites including Rock Paper Shotgun, Eurogamer, and this one, Gamesradar. He particularly enjoys writing about spooky indies, throwback RPGs, and anything that's vaguely silly. Also has an English Literature and Film Studies degree that he'll soon forget. items.title: Jennifer English Has Left Tides Of Annihilation's Main Role To "Slow Down" And "Rest"
items.summary: The role of Gwen has been recast, with English taking on an advisory role.
items.content:
Jennifer English Has Left Tides Of Annihilation's Main Role To "Slow Down" And "Rest" From TheGamer (Written by George Foster) on July 18, 2026 | OpenCritic

Baldur’s Gate 3 , Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 star, and general gaming legend Jennifer English has confirmed that she’s no longer starring in the upcoming Tides of Annihilation so that she can "slow down".

Over the past few years, there have been a few notable rising stars when it comes to acting in games. As much as I love Ben Starr , perhaps the biggest example of this is the wonderful Jennifer English, who rose to fame after playing Shadowheart in Baldur’s Gate 3 ,and then somehow topped that performance with Maelle in Clair Obscur .

Since both of those games ended up winning Game of the Year by a landslide , it’s become a bit of a trend that having English in your game is a very, very good sign . It looked that way for the already promising Tides of Annihilation, but English will no longer star in the game’s lead role and has stepped back to “slow down and reassess” her commitments.

Last night, the official Tides of Annihilation Twitter account shared a statement announcing the “sad news” that English will no longer star as the game’s protagonist, Gwen, due to “personal health reasons”. This decision was made last month, and English...

items.title: Cosplayers Showcased Alicia, Verso, and Clea from Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 - The Dessandr Family of Artists
items.summary: Models Ekaterina "Waters" Kozina, Ilya "Zektornis" Shevchenko, and Anastasia "Svizee" Volkova successfully transformed into Alicia, Verso, and Clea from the role-playing game Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 - members of the talented Dessandr family of artists.
items.content:
0 Источник: Официальная группа фотографа Tanuki на VK.com 09:51 Models Ekaterina "Waters" Kozina, Ilya "Zektornis" Shevchenko, and Anastasia "Svizee" Volkova successfully transformed into Alicia, Verso, and Clea from the role-playing game Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 - members of the talented Dessandr family of artists.

Photographer Alleria "Tanuki" Gegel published new photos intended for fans of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 who have already played this role-playing game.

During the photoshoot, cosplayers Ekaterina "Waters" Kozina, Ilya "Zektornis" Shevchenko, and Anastasia "Svizee" Volkova appeared as characters such as Alicia, Verso, and Clea.

Clea, Verso, and Alicia are key figures belonging to the mysterious and influential Dessandr family. In the game's world, humanity tries to survive and confronts the mysterious Artist.

At the beginning of the story, their relationship is presented as a complex family drama, full of distrust, unspoken words, and old grievances. They are divided not only ideologically but also physically, being on opposite sides of the conflict in a crumbling world.

Verso takes on the role of a secret protector, Clea acts as a strict and powerful figure pursuing grand goals, and Alicia tries to find a balance between them and restore the lost family legacy.

items.title: 'Clair Obscur: Expedition 33' Arm of Gustave: Special Collectible Is Releasing in 2027
items.summary: Clair Obscur is releasing a special collectible, called the Arm of Gustave, will be released next year for fans of Expedition 33
items.content:
Arm of Gustave PureArts/Sandfall Interactive " Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 " is treating fans to a massive new release, a real-life collectible based on one of the most recognizable characters in the game.

Collectible maker PureArts has revealed a life-size replica of Gustave's mechanical arm from "Clair Obscur: Expedition 33," marking the company's first premium piece tied to the acclaimed RPG. The arm is now available for pre-order, with shipping planned for the third quarter of 2027.

PureArts describes the piece as a limited-edition, life-size recreation of the mechanical arm belonging to Gustave, one of the game's central protagonists. Crafted in polyresin, the replica features marbled textures and gold accents meant to reflect the arm's distinct in-game design.

The statue includes rotating combination cylinders, adding an interactive touch that mirrors how the arm functions within "Clair Obscur: Expedition 33." It sits on a sculpted rock base covered in delicate flowers, offering a contrast to the raw power tied to Gustave's Overcharge attack in combat.

PureArts describes the piece as a centerpiece meant to celebrate the game's first anniversary since its original release.

The replica measures roughly 19 inches long, 9 inches tall, and 6 inches deep, weighing about 5.5 pounds once fully assembled.

PureArts has marketed it as a display-focused collectible rather than a toy, aimed squarely at longtime fans and collectors who want a tangible piece tied to one of the game's more recognizable characters.

The Arm of Gustave replica is priced at $429 and comes as a limited edition release, licensed by developer Sandfall Interactive. According to ScreenRant , each unit will be individually numbered on its base plate and paired with a matching edition certificate of authenticity, standard practice for PureArts' premium collectible line.

PureArts has listed a shipping window of the third quarter of 2027 for the piece, meaning collectors who pre-order now will need to wait well over a year before the statue actually arrives.

Pre-orders currently require a 15 percent deposit, with the remaining balance due once the piece is ready to ship.

PureArts has also noted that images shown so far reflect a prototype, meaning the final production version could see minor adjustments before it ships to buyers.

Gustave serves as the primary protagonist of "Clair Obscur: Expedition 33," an engineer who spent his life working to protect the city of Lumière through its defense and agricultural systems.

He is also credited with developing the technology that allows Lumina to be harnessed within the game's world.

Having grown up under the constant threat posed by the Paintress, Gustave eventually joins Expedition 33 as one of its members, dedicating what he believes to be his final year of life toward defeating her and securing a future for Lumière's children.

items.title: New Official Clair Obscur Release Confirmed for 2027
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 developer Sandfall and its partners showcase a new release focused on Gustave coming in 2027.
items.content:
New Official Clair Obscur Release Confirmed for 2027 From GameRant (Written by Joshua Duckworth) on July 16, 2026 | OpenCritic

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has become one of gaming's biggest success stories over the past year, and fans will be able to revisit it in 2027 with a brand-new release. This new release is focused on the best Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 character Gustave, although fans should be aware that it is on the pricier end.

The award-winning RPG quickly earned a passionate fanbase after its breakout launch, and since then, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has remained on everyone's tongue. Everything from merch to new updates has been on the menu for a while, but now fans have something else to look forward to— Clair Obscur: Arm of Gustave .

Guillaume Broche, director of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, recently made some salient and noteworthy comments about Zelda: Breath of the Wild.

The newest collectible to join the world of Expedition 33 is Clair Obscur: Arm of Gustave, a life-size replica of Gustave's iconic mechanical arm produced by PureArts. Measuring 19 inches long, the premium collectible faithfully recreates one of the game's most recognizable objects with a marble-like polyresin finish, metallic gold accents, rotating cylinders, and a detailed display base covered in flowers. It's one of the most elaborate gaming collectibles...

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 wins Best Game at Develop:Star Awards 2026
items.summary: This year's Develop:Star Award recipient was Monument Valley 3 developer Ustwo Games
items.content:
Monument Valley 3 developer Ustwo Games also receives Develop:Star Award

Image credit: Sandfall Interactive News by Sophie McEvoy Staff Writer Published on July 16, 2026 Follow Kepler Interactive The Develop:Star Awards 2026 were held last night, where Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 continued its success by winning three awards.

Sandfall Interactive's title was recognised for Best Game, Best Narrative, and Best Audio.

Frontier Developments was named Best Studio after launching Jurassic World Evolution 3, while Landfall and Aggro Crab's Peak received Best Original IP.

Both developers also jointly received the Best Small Studio award.

Sony Interactive Entertainment received the Publishing Star award, and Sucker Punch Productions' Ghost of Yōtei won Best Visual Art.

No Man's Sky took home the inaugural Best Ongoing Game award, and FuturLab's PowerWash Simulator was recognised for Best Mobile Game.

This year's Develop:Star Award recipient was Monument Valley 3 developer Ustwo Games.

"Thank you to everyone who joined us for The Develop:Star Awards last night and congratulations to all the winners and those shortlisted," said Tandem Events managing director Andy Lane.

"It was a wonderful evening spent celebrating the very best of the amazing work released over the past year, and the amazing people behind that work. Thank you to our sponsors, everyone who voted, and all those who made the night possible, it was an evening to remember."

The Develop:Star Awards will return next year on Wednesday, July 14, during Develop:Brighton 2027, which will be held from July 13 to July 15.

items.title: "Clair Obscur: Expedition 33" has been awarded Best Game at the 2026 Develop:Star Awards
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 maintained its winning streak by securing three awards at the Develop:Star Awards 2026, which took place last night.
items.content:
Back to news "Clair Obscur: Expedition 33" has been awarded Best Game at the 2026 Develop:Star Awards Image credit: Sandfall Interactive Last night's Develop:Star Awards 2026 saw significant wins for the game "Clair Obscur: Expedition 33," which clinched awards in three categories.

Recognized for its excellence, Sandfall Interactive's game was honored with the awards for Best Game, Best Narrative, and Best Audio.

Awarded as the Best Studio, Frontier Developments was celebrated following the release of Jurassic World Evolution 3. The prize for Best Original IP went to Landfall and Aggro Crab for their game, Peak.

These developers also shared the accolade for Best Small Studio.

Sony Interactive Entertainment took home the Publishing Star award, while Ghost of Yōtei, by Sucker Punch Productions, won for Best Visual Art.

No Man's Sky received the first-ever Best Ongoing Game award, and FuturLab's PowerWash Simulator was distinguished as Best Mobile Game.

The prestigious Develop:Star Award was granted to Ustwo Games, known for Monument Valley 3.

Andy Lane, managing director of Tandem Events, expressed gratitude to the attendees, saying, "Thank you to everyone who joined us for The Develop:Star Awards last night and congratulations to all the winners and those shortlisted."

He added, "It was a wonderful evening spent celebrating the very best of the amazing work released over the past year, and the amazing people behind that work. Thank you to our sponsors, everyone who voted, and all those who made the night possible, it was an evening to remember."

The Develop:Star Awards are set to return on Wednesday, July 14, during the 2027 Develop:Brighton event scheduled from July 13 to 15.

items.title: Expedition 33 lead says if people don't like the studio's next game 'that's life'
items.summary: Sandfall Interactive isn't trying to make another GOTY
items.content:
By Willa Rowe Published Jul 2, 2026, 1:05 PM EDT News Sandfall Interactive doesn't seem too worried about living up to GOTY expectations

After GOTY pull, Clair Obscur devs draw line in sand over AI: 'Everything will be made by humans by us' 2026's most anticipated indie horror game just got delayed Final Fantasy 7 Revelation datamining has fans hopeful for DLC Expedition 33 lead says if people don't like the studio's next game 'that's life' Image: Sandfall Interactive/Kepler Interactive Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Sign in to your Polygon.com account Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , the debut game from developer Sandfall Interactive, was a smash hit in 2025 and earned numerous game of the year awards . The studio's next project, whatever it may be, might not be as popular. Director Guillaume Broche knows Clair Obscur is a tough act to follow, and he's okay if no one likes what the studio does next.

In a lengthy episode of Video Game Club on the Konbini YouTube channel, Broche discusses Sandfall's next game and says, "Maybe people won't like it. That's life." It is a surprisingly laid-back attitude toward following up one of the most beloved games in recent memory. For Broche, it isn't any different from the ethos he and the rest of the team at Sandall Interactive had while making Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 . "We didn't make the first game to please anyone," he confesses, "and I think that's why it worked." Rather than pleasing an imaginary audience, Broche reveals that development on Clair Obscur was focused on satisfying the developers. They made a game that excited them. It was just an added bonus that plenty of players enjoyed the game as well.

Guillaume Broche accepts the Game of the Year award at The Game Awards 2025. Image: The Game Awards The process obviously worked, and Broche sees no reason to mess with it, despite mounting expectations brought on by the success of Clair Obscur . He admits that there is definitely pressure to make things bigger or to expand the studio because of the attention Sandfall Interactive now has for its next project. Ultimately, Broche says that this would only result in the studio making games that he doesn't actually want to play.

As for Sandfall's next project, Broche declined to share details but did reveal the team will make some drastic decisions. A big question is whether or not the studio's next game will be a sequel to Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 . In the interview, Broche reiterates the idea he's shared before that "Clair Obscur" is the name of the franchise and Expedition 33 is just one story the team wanted to tell. This could mean Sandfall will take an anthology approach like in Final Fantasy , with each entry introducing a new world and cast of characters. Returning to the world of Expedition 33 in a true sequel would also require Sandfall to choose a canon ending for the game, something the studio has deliberately avoided. In any case, whatever follows Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 will be worth watching.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is available on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and Windows PC.

A recent interview session between Twitch streamers and content creators saw Sandfall Interactive clarifying its AI usage

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is now available in Arabic!
items.summary: Expeditioners! Following the huge demand from so many of you, we’ve been working super hard to be able to finally announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, is now available with Arabic text! This brings the total number of supported langua
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Following the huge demand from so many of you, we’ve been working super hard to be able to finally announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, is now available with Arabic text!

This brings the total number of supported languages to 20!

With the new Arabic language support, we had to consider additional changes to the game’s UI to ensure a smooth and enjoyable experience for Arabic-speaking players around the world.

Adding Arabic localization has been a true team effort over many months, from the incredible adaptation and localization work carried out by the linguists, to reworking the vast majority of the user interface for right-to-left reading, all the way through localization testing.

Every team involved brought passion and dedication to making this possible, and we couldn’t be more grateful for their work. We hope this update makes the story feel even more personal to our players.

Today’s update also includes some additional fixes and improvements. Read the full patch notes here!

Thank you all so much for your continued support! <3

Tomorrow comes.

~ The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: Patch v1.5.6 is now live!

Expeditioners,

Following the huge demand from so many of you, we’ve been working super hard to be able to finally announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is now available in Arabic!

Content

  • New supported language (text): Arabic

    [/*]

  • Credits updated (new linguists, testers and fonts)

    [/*]

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where applying burn after an enemy has been stunned causes them to be able to act immediately after recovering from the stun.

    [/*]

  • Fixed Cleim weapon level 10 Lumina effect that does not trigger correctly on every instance of area of effect skills.

    [/*]

  • Fixed Martenon’s Lumina effects that do not work if Sciel does not have two consecutive turns.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where Sireso weapon, Level 4 Lumina, and Powerful buff, from Pictos/Luminas, applied at the start of battle may override each other depending on the character's position in combat.

    [/*]

  • Fixed Chromatic Lampmaster's AOE skill missing a sound cue on the jump attack.

    [/*]

  • Fixed Sirene's Widow Ballet missing SFX during second charm attack.

    [/*]

  • Fixed characters in combat that may have displaced heads in Photo Mode if they were not seen while turning on Photo Mode.

    [/*]

  • Various minor fixes of the UI and localizations.

    [/*]

We are aware of a crash affecting some of you when loading into the Reacher or going out of the Manor. This crash is related to some specific PC configurations. Until we fix this issue, you can work around it by setting ‘Global Illumination’ to anything below ‘Epic’ temporarily.

As always, thanks for your support and for your reports via the official Typeform - We'll continue monitoring the reports coming in and working on future fixes!

~ The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 New Update 1.500.600 on June 30 Gives More Devs Their Flowers
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has been updated to version 1.5.6 and appears as download 1.500.600 on consoles and adds a few small suff.
items.content:
While Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is already feature-complete as a single-player, narrative-driven RPG, that hasn’t stopped developer Sandfall Interactive from releasing countless updates to add to the experience.

Today, a new game update has been released, and while it’s not a major patch, it’s something game developers — and even some gamers — will appreciate.

Players will be able to download Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 update 1.500.600 for version 1.5.6 today, June 30 , and this adds more developers to the credits section, and also slots in a few bug fixes too.

For the complete info, read on for what Sandfall has to say.

Sandfall has acknowledged a crash that occurs when loading into the Reacher, and has issued a workaround:

According to the developers, the crash only affects certain PC hardware or software configurations.

This only affects the PC version, and we’re hoping a patch for it is released soon.

While nothing has been announced officially, the developers did confirm that more games in the franchise are on the way.

RELATED Microsoft Rejects Reports That PS5 GTA 6 Preorders Are Beating Xbox 8 to 1 In the past few hours, reports claimed that the PS5 version of GTA 6 was getting far more preorders than the Xbox version, with an estimated six-to-one gap.

Stay connected to MP1st and the latest news by following us on Bluesky , X , Facebook , TikTok , YouTube , and Google News .

Father, gamer, games media vet, writer of words, killer of noobs.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 x Dispatch Bundle
items.summary: Expeditioners! Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and Dispatch are now available together in a 10% off bundle! Bundle art by Derek Stratton This is a really special bundle for us - since its release, Dispatch left such an impact on us and immediat
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and Dispatch are now available together in a 10% off bundle! 

Bundle art by Derek Stratton

This is a really special bundle for us - since its release, Dispatch left such an impact on us and immediately became one of our favourites.   

Our games are quite different, but we couldn’t help ourselves - and ultimately, we just want more people to discover the powerful stories and artistry behind these games.   

So in the words of our Creative Director, Guillaume Broche: “Play Dispatch, it’s very very cool”

Save 10% off both games by purchasing this bundle!

items.title: 1st Anniversary of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
items.summary: Expeditioners!! Today marks 1 year since we released Clair Obscur: Expedition 33. This past year has been an incredible journey and so we have a few things to share with you today. To celebrate the occasion, the team put together a special
items.content:
Expeditioners!! 

 

Today marks 1 year since we released Clair Obscur: Expedition 33. This past year has been an incredible journey and so we have a few things to share with you today. 

 

To celebrate the occasion, the team put together a special piece of artwork that we hope you will enjoy! 

8 Million Copies Sold

We're so thrilled, and honestly still quite overwhelmed, to announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has officially reached 8 million copies sold!

Thank you so so much to every single person who has played, created art, shared, streamed, discussed, and supported this game!!

Game Update: New Haircuts + Patch Notes

We've pushed a fun little update live today: new haircuts are now available in-game, giving the expedition even more style to strut around in! 💅 

For the full list of changes, fixes, and improvements, you can read the patch notes here

Community Emotes

Our community is packed with phenomenally talented artists and we wanted to celebrate that today.  

We commissioned some of our amazing community creators to design official Expedition 33 emotes for you to use in your own channels! 

A massive thank you to IrunoArt, comickergirl (Sarah Leuver), and Bugyakis for their brilliant work on each of these!!

They're FREE to download and use here

Official Baguette Merch

In the mood to add some baguette to your wardrobe? 

We’ve partnered with Orcival and Le Béret Français to bring you the official Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 striped shirt and beret for your cosplays! 🥖    You can purchase the official Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 x Orcival mariniere here

And the official Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 x Le Béret Français beret is available to buy here

On top of that, keep an eye out for a limited number of units of the Monolith Set to be back in stock from our partners at Laced and Pix n' Love soon!! 

And Finally… Cake

It's been a big day for us. Thank you for making it possible. 🎂 

And if you know someone who wants to pick up the game, we're running a 20% sale right now on Steam - As well as some fun merchandise goodies available on our merch page!

Merch Page

From the bottom of our hearts, we’re so thankful to each and every one of you – this has been the best year of our lives, thanks to you. 

 

- The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: Patch v1.5.5 is now LIVE!
items.summary: Expeditioners! Happy 1 Year Anniversary of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33!! The details of this patch are written out below: New content Added 1st Anniversary haircuts for the expedition! Gustave's Anniversary haircut is available at the Gestr

    • We've noticed something went wrong after adjusting this, we're investigating now to resolve the issue

      [/*]

    • Fixed the issue! We've readjusted some of the achievement descriptions back to the correct achievements. Thank you for your patience!!

      [/*]
    [/*]

As always, thanks for your support and for your reports via the official Typeform - and most importantly, thank you for all of your support this entire year!! We wish you a wonderful anniversary day and hope you enjoy some cake to celebrate with us!

~ The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: The developers of Clair Obscur gave me an interview and some advice
items.summary: We spoke about the game's finale (don't worry, there are spoiler warnings), the best Pictos, and what I can be doing better.
items.content:
Stephen Totilo Mar 27, 2026 35 3 1 Share Clair Obscur: Expedition 33. Screenshot: Sandfall Interactive, captured by Game File This story, perhaps appropriately, goes on a bit longer than you might expect, past what may feel like the conclusion.

This was not by design. In my business of reporting, you can’t plan surprise endings. But it is what delightfully happened.

This story is merely an accurate account of my absurd endeavor from earlier this month, when I tried to finally play through 2025’s most acclaimed video game, the role-playing phenomenon Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , in a sudden rush.

I had a deadline: a date when I expected to have a chance to chat about Clair Obscur with some of its creators. We would perhaps discuss the game’s ending, which, in their business of making video games, they could plan for.

I will get to all of that. I will get to Thursday night, March 12, when I spoke to two of Clair Obscur’s creators, discussed the ending and got myself an in-person consultation about my characters’ builds.

It’s Sunday, March 8 and I have downloaded Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 to the portable ROG Xbox Ally X , a Switch-looking device that can run games offered via Xbox Game Pass. At this point, I have only played a little past the prologue of Clair Obscur, back on an Xbox in my house. Cloud saving has transferred my progress to the portable. In the game, our protagonist Gustave and his sort-of sister Maelle have set out on Expedition 33, the latest annual beach landing of adventurers who hope they will be the ones to take down the distant and mysterious paintress, who somehow, magically annihilates everyone of a certain age each year.

I know I’ve given myself barely enough time to play through this game ahead of Thursday, when Clair Obscur is likely to win another pile of awards at the former Game Developers Conference (now GDC Festival of Gaming) and when I’ll be backstage with an opportunity to interview the winners.

In the weeks prior, I’ve been busy writing and reporting and, in terms of actually using a controller, playing through Resident Evils .

Just back in February, I had been backstage at a different gaming awards event, where I’d met some of the makers of Clair Obscur and where I’d not spoken to them about the game, because I had other coverage goals for my chats with the winners.

For the Game Developers Choice Awards on March 12, though, I want to talk about the games. My friend urges me on. You should try to finish Clair Obscur, he says. They haven’t spoken about the ending all that much, yet it’s all the game’s fans talk about. You gotta find out what happens, he says, even if you just have to watch a video. Perish the thought.

So I’m on United Airlines flight 1777 from EWR to SFO, comfortably seated in window seat 39A. My Ally X is in my hands, and Clair Obscur is running, well before takeoff. I’ve boarded fairly early. Soon, a man with long curly hair takes the middle seat next to me. There is still plenty of elbow room for me, and I start playing. Best I can tell, the man in the middle seat soon starts a nap.

Gustave, alongside a magically powered woman named Lune, are fighting enemies called Nevrons in dramatic turn-based fights. They reunite with red-haired Maelle. As the plane takes off and begins to cross the United States, things are going well. I’m loving the combat and the look of the game’s world.

Before my plane lands, I reach the end of Clair Obscur’s first act. Big twist! I’ve been rushing, avoiding sidequests, and, wow. I did not see that coming. I’m stunned, but I don’t think anyone on the flight notices. I make sure not to disturb the curly-haired guy next to me, who I think at this point is watching a movie?

After my flight lands, I text my friend to confirm that I have not seen Clair Obscur’s wildest turn. I have not.

items.title: Patch v1.5.3 is live!
items.summary: Expeditioners! Thank you for all of your reports - the details for the fixes in this patch are written out below: Update On Sirene Crash After a thorough internal investigation, the crash that occurred after defeating Sirene can no longer b
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Thank you for all of your reports - the details for the fixes in this patch are written out below:

Update On Sirene Crash

  • After a thorough internal investigation, the crash that occurred after defeating Sirene can no longer be reproduced on our end. If you keep getting this issue, we’d suggest updating your Windows to the latest version. If the issue persists, please submit a report and we’ll continue investigating.

    [/*]

Bug Fixes & Improvements

Photo Mode

  • Fixed facial hair of characters being blurred in the Photo Mode on the close distance if Shading was set to Epic.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in Photo mode that prevented the Inner Cone Angle and Outer Cone Angle options from affecting the shape of the preview light source.

    [/*]

  • Photo Mode's spotlight and point light display has been modified to improve their visibility

    [/*]

Gameplay

  • Fixed an issue where loading a save while picking up a journal could cause that journal to disappear permanently and become impossible to collect again.

    • Missing journals are now also restored retroactively when loading affected saves.

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • Fixed a soft lock that could occur, during the Chromatic Glissando encounter in Sirene’s Dress level, when a dead expeditioner equipped with Shortcut and Second Chance pictos was swallowed.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on Gestral Beach where using the Rematch option after losing the volleyball minigame could create a blocked save.

    [/*]

  • Fixed a visual issue where characters affected by Inverted would not have the correct colour effect applied to their eyes.

    [/*]

Environment

  • Fixed various environment issues (collisions, misplaced assets, missing or incorrect footsteps SFX) across Verso's Drafts, the World Map, and other levels.

    [/*]

  • Fixed various lighting issues on Steam Deck in Verso’s Drafts, Endless Tower and Stone Wave Cliffs levels.

    [/*]

As always, thanks for your support and for your reports via the official Typeform - We'll continue monitoring the reports coming in and working on future fixes!

~ The Expedition 33 Team
